title: allium flavum subsp. tauricum
intro: Allium flavum subsp. tauricum, also known as the golden garlic or tauricum onion, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its yellowish-orange flowers and onion-like leaves. The plant is said to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Allium flavum subsp. tauricum include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve heart health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or capsule, and is also used in folk remedies to aid digestion and relieve cold symptoms.
